summary: iter33 — Verified the polling/completion visibility fix. All 30 unit tests in test_avatar_lipsync_image_upload.py pass (including the 3 new iter33 tests: progress_dict_populated_on_completed, completed_with_error_returns_render_exception, result_alternative_video_url_shapes). All 3 new live integration tests in test_avatar_iter33_diagnostics.py pass against PREVIEW — confirming the 8 new diagnostic fields (provider_request_id, provider_status, poll_attempts, last_poll_at, fal_endpoint, final_result_keys, failure_reason, completed_at, video_url_present) are present in /api/avatar-chat/send response.message AND /api/avatar-chat/job/{message_id} response.message. failure_reason is populated (PROVIDER_AUTH_FAILED) and video_url_present is false on preview as expected. Code review of avatar_chat.py confirms all 6 requested changes are in place. Frontend smoke test: admin /video-avatar-chat renders the existing rose error_code chip + the show-provider-response debug panel; the new amber provider_status and muted req:<id> chips correctly do NOT render because provider_status/provider_request_id are null on preview (no FAL_KEY). No new JS console errors.

avatar_chat.py:603-619 — polling loop calls handler.status() in a while loop with _t.sleep(LIPSYNC_POLL_INTERVAL_SEC), not handler.get() with a timeout. ✓
avatar_chat.py:632-645 — Completed-with-error branch surfaces RENDER_EXCEPTION with detail provider_completed_with_error:<type>:<message>. ✓
avatar_chat.py:660-674 — alternate result-body shapes ({video:{url}, {video:str}, {video_url}, {output_video_url}) handled via _pick_first_url. ✓
avatar_chat.py:849-873 _persist_progress coroutine runs concurrently, snapshots progress dict every 2s into db.avatar_chat_messages, then cancel/final-flush after helper returns. ✓
avatar_chat.py:975-998 _public_message exposes all 8 new diagnostic fields (verified live on preview — all keys present in send/job responses). ✓
avatar_chat.py:955-959 success path now also sets video_url_present:true, failure_reason:null. ✓
avatar_chat.py:887-892 audio-only fallback persistence sets failure_reason=error_code, video_url_present=False. ✓ (Live preview confirms failure_reason='PROVIDER_AUTH_FAILED' when FAL_KEY missing.)

frontend VideoAvatarChat.jsx:74-92 — admin-only amber provider_status chip and muted req:<8chars> chip both implemented with correct data-testids (avatar-provider-status-<id> and avatar-provider-req-<id>) and gated on isAdmin && field-non-null. ✓
Non-blocking: avatar_chat.py is now 1424 lines — still over the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting in a future iteration (mentioned in iter32 review).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_avatar_iter33_diagnostics.py (new — 3 live integration tests against preview verifying the 8 new diagnostic fields)
test_credentials: Admin krajapraveen@gmail.com / TestPass123! verified working via /api/auth/login on preview. Preview has NO FAL_KEY (expected); polling logic exercised via unit tests with mocked fal_client.
seed_data_creation: None. Used existing seeded demo clone 'raja-demo' (clone_ffc5c58241e944) for live send/job tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter33 fully verified. Polling/completion observability now confirmed end-to-end: backend persists provider_request_id/provider_status/poll_attempts/last_poll_at/fal_endpoint/final_result_keys/failure_reason/completed_at/video_url_present into db.avatar_chat_messages, and these fields surface in both /send (immediate response) and /job/{message_id} (poll endpoint). Frontend admin chips (data-testid=avatar-provider-status-<id> and avatar-provider-req-<id>) are correctly gated on isAdmin && field-non-null. On preview they stay hidden because FAL_KEY is missing → progress dict never populated. To exercise the live polling chips, the main agent must set FAL_KEY in PRODUCTION .env, then send a chat — admin should see Queued ×N → InProgress ×N → Completed ×N tick up in real time. Pre-existing 3 unrelated failures in test_avatar_chat.py (subscription gate) remain untouched.
rca of the issue: iter32 confirmed the fal model ID was correct (sadtalker live) but iter32-era code blocked on handler.get() with a 180s ceiling — no visibility into whether fal was actually receiving the request, what its job status was, or whether it eventually completed. iter33 replaces the blocking call with an explicit handler.status() polling loop (5s default interval, 300s timeout), shares a progress dict between the worker thread and the asyncio event loop, and runs a 2s persister coroutine that snapshots the dict into MongoDB. The frontend now polls /job every 2s and renders the live status. RENDER_EXCEPTION is now surfaced for sadtalker's Completed-with-error case (face detection failures), and 4 alternate result-body shapes are accepted. Mitigation already complete — no further main-agent action required.
